title: Retail: 1998 Industry Practice Summary
author: Aberdeen Group
abstract: Aberdeen Group's 1998 survey of IT investments and trends in the U.S. retail sector, covering point-of-sale systems, supply chain management, data warehousing, e-commerce, and Y2K readiness. The report identifies integration of disparate systems as the defining IT challenge for retailers, and forecasts that data warehousing and Internet commerce will become competitive necessities. Supplier profiles cover 19 vendors including IBM, NCR, SAP, and Andersen Consulting.
importance_rationale: Institutional practice summary documenting Aberdeen's retail IT coverage scope; not primary research but a useful snapshot of 1998 retail IT vendor landscape.
relevance_rationale: Retail omnichannel IT and supply-chain integration remain core enterprise IT topics; e-commerce predictions proved foundational.
prescience_rationale: Aberdeen's forecasts that Internet commerce would become a mandatory retail channel and that data warehousing would shift from 'icing' to necessity proved highly accurate.
